Service identification: Service Cegeka Cloud – Housing Services Unique ID PRD.0.L.101 Service Model Infrastructure management This service is a base service and is not dependant of other layers (and not dependant of other Service Descriptions).
Infrastructure management: The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage the Cegeka Cloud Housing (The Infrastructure) from a technical perspective. Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects for this infrastructure. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.
The Infrastructure: The Cegeka Housing Service offers physical locations for the installation of hardware in the Cegeka datacentres. The housing service comes with three flavors: Shared rack spaceClosed dedicated rack The physical location can be used to house Customer dedicated equipment (servers, network equipment, storage, etc.) and can provide redundant power, redundant network and redundant SAN connectivity. Customer dedicated equipment: The housing services come with following features: The physical location for the housing 24/7 access control and security of the building and housing location Redundant electrical installation (2 outlets) of the housing (electric distribution, generator, UPS system) Inline UPS system that also protects the IT equipment from power surges and power peaks Redundant climate control (heating, ventilation and air conditioning) of the housing – server inlet 22° ±2°, Relative humidity: 45% ± 15% Fire detection and extinguishers of the housing – based on inert gas, not harmful for humans/equipment Optional office space for the purpose of fallback in case of disaster recovery Optional use of bladecenter infrastructure for the housing of Customer provided server blades Certified Cegeka Datacentre personnel for all aspects above. Cegeka Datacentres are geographically spread over 3 locations: two in Belgium (Hasselt) and Brussels (BRU4). BRU4 location is private cage only Cegeka authorized personnel, and one in the Netherlands (Geleen), Cegeka has controls in her ISAE3000 framework to ensure Cegeka meets (and exceeds) the requirements in the Uptime Tier-III standard , guaranteeing high availability, redundancy, operational continuity and safety and security. Cegeka provides also additional connectivity POP (Point of Presence) locations in Brussels (Zaventem) and the Amsterdam (Oude Meer), where Customer network access endpoint equipment can be placed. The Cegeka Datacentres and POPs are interconnected by Cegeka private and redundant data connection lines. Bandwidth is continuously and pro-actively monitored. Cegeka will take appropriate actions such as increasing the total available bandwidth for all customers as part of its capacity management process. Please note that the locations and interconnection of the Cegeka Datacentres and POPs may change over time. Such changes are always communicated upfront to relevant Customers and do not impact the nature of this Service.
Standard Service features: Cegeka ensures skilled and certified engineers with experience on all housing components in order to perform the Service. Cegeka works together with contracted vendors to provide continuity on these components. Cegeka continue evaluates and adapts the housing environment to ensure optimal technical setup (racks, cabling, physical security measurements, power supply, etc.). Cegeka advises the Customer on housing needs and proposes optimal solutions for each case. Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Infrastructure ensuring its correct working in accordance with Tier 3 Datacentre standards. Technical setup is documented on operational level but is not available to the Customer. It can be subject to formal Customer auditing upon prior announcement. Documents can be shown but are not distributed. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Examples of technical management activities: Cooling, Generators, Low tension, high tension, UPS - maintenance activities Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Cegeka consider following housekeeping tasks: cable management, environmental monitoring and maintenance, PUDs inspections, fire suppression equipment. Proactive management Cegeka performs housekeeping activities on all aspects of the housing service. Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the housing infrastructure on a 24/7 basis (power, climate, access, etc.) Alerts are treated and appropriate resolution actions are triggered and followed to assure the SLA. Physical security The housing locations and the Cegeka Datacentre and POP locations are permanent physically secured and monitored. Access is strictly arranged by the Cegeka Access Control procedure: Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. . The Customer remains responsible for the equipment placed in the housing locations. This may be by the Customer itself, a Third Party or by an adjacent Cegeka Service.
Optional Service features: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. There is No self service access. A Data Center engineer has to validate all accesses. The following optional features are possible with the Service:
Requests: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). Additional or complex requests from the Customer will be treated as Projects. request type Description Lead time Service Requests Access to a Cegeka Datacentre or POP for hardware installation or service purposes (Engineers) 24 hours Physical handling (power on/off, status/led check, etc) 1 hour Normal Changes Any other request. Examples: •	Advise on housing •	Hands-on assistance for hardware handling or connectivity handling (power, network, SAN, etc.) •	Shipping, device handling, packaging, etc. Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.
Out of scope: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service.
Obligatory: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:
CMDBCegeka owned Licenses and/or subscri: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to (none specific)
Customer owned Licenses and/or subscript: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks (none specific)
Conditions: For the usage of rack space, the equipment must be industry standard 19” rack compatible For the usage of server blades, the equipment must be compatible with the Cegeka blade infrastructure (to be discussed on a need basis) Equipment must have a front to back airflow. Equipment must be 230V or 400V (3 phases, max 32A) or 230V (1 phase, max 32A)
Service delivery location: This Service is delivered from within the physical Cegeka Datacentre and POP locations.
Service Reporting: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required. Specific reports are provided:
Data classification: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Cegeka Datacentre personnel may see fragments of data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of information on screens, etc.)
Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): (none) Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): Penta Datacenter Geleen (DC Geleen)
